reviews.review_msg: Dr. Krider adapted this course to online very well. Weekly labs/assignments/readings were reasonable length and well connected. The R programming was well explained and shouldn't scare you away. The marking was timely and fair.

My favourite prof at SFU so far (by far). He is engaging, has witty humour, and knows his stuff. The course isnt easy, reading through the tutorials and weekly readings is a MUST. Weekly quizzes are based on readings, and fair. 2 large projects, dont underestimate the time these will take. He's a fair grader, but you will need to work for marks.

I learned a lot from Robert in BUS 446 (Marketing Strategy). He is a very knowledgable prof with a good attitude toward helping students. Lectures are engaging as the majority is spent in class discussion about the weeks case reading. No exams, only projects and assignments. Not easy, but fair, marking.

Great guy, interesting course. Provides a lot of real world examples, and takes polls in class to see different attitudes towards different aspects of marketing. Good prof to take marketing with if you want a good grade!
Useless!!  He doesn't know what he's talking about, there was always something wrong with his lectures, and exam questions were too vague and general! yuck!

Classes weren't useful but you have to go because videos shown in class are tested. Exams are ALL multiple choice. Know all the definitions in the chapters that are tested. Most marking really depends on your TA, so if you get a more lenient TA, then you're in luck. You really don't learn much in class, and only the videos are interesting.
The class requires a lot of work! The key point is you have to be in a good group. The prof is okay overall, but I like my TA better. The contents are not much useful, from my perspective.
The class in general is A LOT of work, but Krider does a good job of communicating the material.  The exams are really fair and pretty much straight out of the textbook.  Definitely recommend him.

Not sure why the previous poster feels that way. While not the most dynamic speaker, he gets the point across well enough. The class structure is fairly repetitive in that material is repeated in the text, lecture and lab, but it helps reinforce your skills. Probably the least demanding 400-level class. No surprises on assignments or exams.

Useless professor. I dont have a single good thing to say about him. If you want to take this class i suggest a different professor Jason Ho would be 50 times better. This guy doesnt know a thing about the class he teaches. Useless
